mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to equity cash flows, mechanically lifting present valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in labor market conditions signals an impending economic contraction, triggering risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day forward returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 45% over a 3-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent strength in consumer credit expansion and housing starts reflects robust aggregate demand, which supports corporate earnings growth and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict returns with a Rank IC above 0.05 over any 12-month rolling window, or forward returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero when both proxies rise simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates expanding supply capacity that outpaces demand growth, compressing input costs and boosting corporate margins, which yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns turn negative when INDPRO rises above its 12-month moving average, or the signal's predictive power vanishes during periods of supply chain disruption.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real GDP growth directly increases corporate revenue expectations, leading to immediate equity repricing and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The rolling Spearman correlation between quarterly GDP growth surprises and 5-day forward SPX returns remains below 0.10 over a 5-year sample, or forward returns show no significant difference between positive and negative GDP growth quarters.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5%, tight monetary conditions restrict liquidity and elevate borrowing costs, suppressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in > 55% of observations where FEDFUNDS stays above 4.5% for consecutive months, or the signal's directional accuracy falls below chance over a 3-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous payroll growth deceleration and unemployment rate elevation confirm a labor market cooling cycle, which reduces consumer spending power and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to generate a statistically significant negative mean forward return (p > 0.05) when PAYEMS growth falls below 100k and UNRATE rises above 4.0%, or the interaction term shows zero predictive power in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery environment, rising consumer credit balances indicate pent-up demand normalization, which rapidly lifts earnings expectations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative or flat when RSAFS expands by more than 2% month-over-month during the specified regime, or the signal's hit rate drops below 40% over a 2-year out-of-sample evaluation.
